title: Add OCI Image Spec annotations to all container images
description:
**Size:** ЁЯФз small
**Category:** infrastructure
**Priority:** medium
**Source:** Proposed for 'Currently no OCI labels are being set on the container'

Add standard OCI annotations to python-base, venv-builder, and keystone container images using a two-layer approach:

1. **CI-generated labels via `docker/metadata-action@v5`** (3 new steps in `.github/workflows/build-images.yaml`):
   - `meta-python-base` step in `build-base-images` job before `build-python-base` step, generating OCI labels (title, description, source, url, version, created, revision, licenses, vendor)
   - `meta-venv-builder` step in `build-base-images` job before `build-venv-builder` step, same label set
   - `meta-keystone` step in `build-service-images` job before `build-service` step, with `version` overridden to `${{ steps.source-ref.outputs.ref }}` (upstream release version from source-refs.yaml) via `raw` version strategy
   - Wire each metadata step's `labels` output into the corresponding `build-push-action` step via `labels: ${{ steps.<meta-step-id>.outputs.labels }}`

2. **Static `LABEL` instructions in Dockerfiles** (3 files):
   - Add `org.opencontainers.image.title`, `.description`, `.licenses=Apache-2.0`, `.vendor` as static LABELs to `images/python-base/Dockerfile`, `images/venv-builder/Dockerfile`, and `images/keystone/Dockerfile`
   - These provide baseline metadata for local builds; CI-generated labels override them at push time

Excluded: tag logic changes, custom/vendor-specific labels, base.name/base.digest annotations, Makefile changes, verify job changes.

**Rationale:** Container images currently ship with zero metadata labels. This makes it impossible to trace a running image back to its source commit, repository, license, or build timestamp without external tooling. OCI annotations are the industry standard adopted by registries (Docker Hub, GHCR, Quay), vulnerability scanners, and audit tools. Adding them improves supply chain transparency, simplifies incident response (which commit produced this image?), and aligns with container best practices at minimal cost.

**Affected Areas:**
- .github/workflows/build-images.yaml
- images/python-base/Dockerfile
- images/venv-builder/Dockerfile
- images/keystone/Dockerfile
elaboration:
**Size:** ЁЯФз small **Category:** infrastructure **Priority:** medium ## Summary Add [OCI Image Spec annotations](https://specs.opencontainers.org/image-spec/annotations/) to all container images (`python-base`, `venv-builder`, `keystone`). Standard labels тАФ title, description, source URL, version, creation timestamp, Git revision, license, and vendor тАФ will be generated dynamically in CI using `docker/metadata-action` and supplemented with static labels in Dockerfiles for local build visibility. ## Scope **Included:** - Add `docker/metadata-action` steps to `build-images.yaml`: two in `build-base-images` (one for `python-base`, one for `venv-builder`) and one in `build-service-images` (for `keystone` / matrix services) - Generate standard OCI annotations: `title`, `description`, `url`, `source`, `version`, `created`, `revision`, `licenses`, `vendor`, `documentation` - Wire metadata-action `labels` output into the existing `docker/build-push-action` steps (`build-python-base`, `build-venv-builder`, `build-service`) via their `labels` input - Add static `LABEL` instructions to all three Dockerfiles (`images/python-base/Dockerfile`, `images/venv-builder/Dockerfile`, `images/keystone/Dockerfile`) for `title`, `description`, `licenses`, `vendor` - For `keystone`: override `org.opencontainers.image.version` with the upstream release version from `releases//source-refs.yaml` (already resolved as `steps.source-ref.outputs.ref` / `VERSION` in the workflow) **Excluded:** - Replacing the existing tag derivation logic (composite/version/SHA tags remain as-is) тАФ YAGNI, tags work correctly today - Custom or vendor-specific labels beyond the OCI standard set тАФ YAGNI, no current consumer - Adding `org.opencontainers.image.base.name` / `.base.digest` annotations тАФ would require cross-job plumbing of base image digests; can be a follow-up if needed - Modifying the `Makefile` `docker-build` target (it's a stub that errors out) - Changes to `verify-base-images` or `verify-service-images` jobs тАФ they pull by digest/tag, not affected by labels ## Visualization ```mermaid flowchart TD subgraph job1["build-base-images job"] direction TB meta1["docker/metadata-action
python-base labels"] build1["build-push-action
#build-python-base"] meta1 -->|"labels output"| build1 meta2["docker/metadata-action
venv-builder labels"] build2["build-push-action
#build-venv-builder"] meta2 -->|"labels output"| build2 end subgraph job2["build-service-images job"] direction TB meta3["docker/metadata-action
keystone labels"] build3["build-push-action
#build-service"] meta3 -->|"labels output"| build3 end subgraph sources["Label Sources"] gh["GitHub Context
sha, repo URL, timestamp"] df["Dockerfile static LABELs
title, description, license, vendor"] rel["source-refs.yaml
upstream version"] end gh --> meta1 gh --> meta2 gh --> meta3 rel -->|"version override"| meta3 df --> build1 df --> build2 df --> build3 subgraph output["Pushed Images"] oci["OCI Annotations
on manifest + config"] end build1 --> oci build2 --> oci build3 --> oci ``` ## Key Components - **`docker/metadata-action` steps** (3 new steps): one before each `build-push-action` тАФ generates OCI-compliant labels from GitHub Actions context; each configured with image-specific `title` and `description`; keystone step additionally overrides `version` from `steps.source-ref.outputs.ref` - **`build-push-action` label wiring** (3 existing steps modified): add `labels: ${{ steps..outputs.labels }}` to `build-python-base`, `build-venv-builder`, and `build-service` - **Dockerfile `LABEL` instructions** (3 files modified): static `org.opencontainers.image.title`, `.description`, `.licenses`, `.vendor` in `images/python-base/Dockerfile`, `images/venv-builder/Dockerfile`, `images/keystone/Dockerfile` тАФ CI-generated labels override these at push time - **Keystone version override**: `docker/metadata-action` for keystone uses a `raw` version strategy set to `${{ steps.source-ref.outputs.ref }}` (e.g. `28.0.0`) instead of the default Git-derived version
